meta.description: ALL enriched hunt cards — compressed detection rules with verification and triage context for direct bug validation
meta.usage: Load this single file for all vulnerability detection cards. For each card: grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly against grep hit locations, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: ERC-7579 Module System — Registry Bypass, moduleType Confusion, Hook PostCheck Skip, Fallback Flaws
cards.detect: ERC-7579 module management has multiple independent failure modes:
check: VERIFY: ERC-7579 module management has multiple independent failure modes
PROVE: *Scenario A — Registry bypass via compromised module [MOD-1]; Attacker submits a malicious module that passes ERC-7484 attestation checks at install time.
ANTIPATTERN: Fallback handler can be called directly by anyone via the fallback function
cards.antipattern: Fallback handler can be called directly by anyone via the fallback function
cards.validWhen: ERC-7579 module management has multiple independent failure modes

cards.title: AA Paymaster Gas Accounting — Prefund Errors, Duplicate Snapshots, Stake Bypass, and Fee Escape
cards.detect: Gas accounting in ERC-4337 is a multi-phase process (validation → execution → postOp) with several hand-off points...
check: VERIFY: Gas accounting in ERC-4337 is a multi-phase process (validation → execution → postOp) with several hand-off points where gas values can be measured...
PROVE: *Scenario A — Inverted prefund multiplier [GAS-1] (OpenZeppelin EIP-4337 audit, HIGH); An op WITHOUT a paymaster submits verificationGasLimit = G.
ANTIPATTERN: preGas reused from before-validation for before-postOp measurement
cards.antipattern: preGas reused from before-validation for before-postOp measurement
cards.validWhen: Gas accounting in ERC-4337 is a multi-phase process (validation → execution → postOp) with several hand-off points where gas values can be measured incorrectly

cards.antipattern: permissionId decoded from userOp.signature which is NOT signed by EntryPoint

cards.validWhen: Both getBuyPrice and getSellPrice use IBondingCurve(bondingCurve).getPriceAndFee with the current tokenCount as the start index.
cards.invalidWhen: Add minAmountOut parameter for slippage protection
cards.impact: User can lose funds.

cards.title: Vulnerable Pattern Examples

cards.securePattern: Implement a 1 block freeze between buying and selling
cards.validWhen: Before swapping for meme tokens using uni.swapExactTokensForTokens(), a minTokens amount is calculated.
cards.invalidWhen: Implement a 1 block freeze between buying and selling
cards.impact: Attacker can drain protocol tokens — PoC showed $1,244,330 profit via setPositionWidth and $548,527 profit via unpause.

cards.title: 1. Proof Forgery via Missing Bounds Checks
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because **Merkle proof indices lack proper size validation**.
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: State variable updated after external interaction instead of before (CEI violation); Signal 2: Withdrawal path produces different...
PROVE: Merkle library returns true for empty proofs when leaf == root
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Standard security patterns (access control, reentrancy guards, input validation) are in place; Safe if: Protocol behavior matches documented...
IMPACT: Forged withdrawals from any EigenPod (CRITICAL)
Are ALL sub-indexes in Merkle proof validation bounds-checked against their tree heights?
Does the Merkle library require non-empty proofs?
cards.antipattern: Merkle library returns true for empty proofs when leaf == root
cards.securePattern: Validate every sub-index in the combined Merkle path
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: State variable updated after external interaction instead of before (CEI violation); Signal 2: Withdrawal path produces different accounting than deposit path...
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Standard security patterns (access control, reentrancy guards, input validation) are in place; Safe if: Protocol behavior matches documented...

cards.title: 2. Beacon State Root Upgrade Breakage
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because **Merkle tree heights and SSZ container layouts are hardcoded** as constants.
check: VERIFY: Merkle tree heights and SSZ container layouts are hardcoded as constants.
PROVE: Zero balance during pending deposit window marks validator as WITHDRAWN
FALSIFY: Require non-zero balance before marking as withdrawn
IMPACT: Complete proof verification failure after consensus upgrades (5/18 reports)
Are ALL tree height constants fork-aware (Deneb, Electra, future forks)?
Are both `0x01` and `0x02` withdrawal credential types supported?
cards.antipattern: Zero balance during pending deposit window marks validator as WITHDRAWN
cards.securePattern: Require non-zero balance before marking as withdrawn
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because Merkle tree heights and SSZ container layouts are hardcoded as constants.
cards.invalidWhen: Require non-zero balance before marking as withdrawn
cards.impact: Complete proof verification failure after consensus upgrades (5/18 reports)

cards.title: 3. stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH Accounting Bugs

PROVE: Increment by 32 ETH, decrement by effective balance
FALSIFY: Decrement by 32 ETH per validator, not by effective balance
IMPACT: Inflated totalETHInPool and consequently inflated rsETH/LRT price (3/18 reports)
Is `stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH` decremented on EVERY path that changes staking state?
Is the decrement amount symmetric with the increment (32 ETH per validator)?
cards.antipattern: Increment by 32 ETH, decrement by effective balance
cards.securePattern: Decrement by 32 ETH per validator, not by effective balance
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH tracks only one direction of the validator lifecycle.
cards.invalidWhen: Decrement by 32 ETH per validator, not by effective balance
cards.impact: Inflated totalETHInPool and consequently inflated rsETH/LRT price (3/18 reports)

cards.title: Section 7: Checkpoint & Voting Power Decay Bugs
cards.detect: VotingEscrow uses checkpoint systems to record point-in-time voting power that decays linearly over lock duration.
check: VERIFY: VotingEscrow uses checkpoint systems to record point-in-time voting power that decays linearly over lock duration.

ANTIPATTERN: MULTIPLIER=2 is too small — blockSlope always rounds to zero
cards.antipattern: MULTIPLIER=2 is too small — blockSlope always rounds to zero
cards.validWhen: VotingEscrow uses checkpoint systems to record point-in-time voting power that decays linearly over lock duration.

cards.title: 1. UID Leak / Object Identity Bypass
cards.detect: The verifier does not validate structures without a `key` capability.

PROVE: Publish a module with struct Bar without key ability — verifier skips UID leak checks; Upgrade the module, adding key ability to Bar
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Standard security patterns (access control, reentrancy guards, input validation) are in place; Safe if: Protocol behavior matches documented...

Verify all structs with UID field have `key` ability from initial publication
Check upgrade compatibility — no ability additions that bypass verifier

cards.securePattern: Verifier should reject adding key ability to existing structs during upgrade
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: State variable updated after external interaction instead of before (CEI violation); Signal 2: Withdrawal path produces different accounting than deposit path...

cards.title: 2. Dynamic Field Hash Collision
cards.detect: The `hash_type_and_key` function concatenates `parent`, `k_bytes`, and `k_tag_bytes` without inserting length delimiters.
check: VERIFY: hash_type_and_key function concatenates parent, k_bytes, and k_tag_bytes without inserting length delimiters.
PROVE: Attacker crafts two different dynamic field type+key combinations that produce the same hash; Operations like add and exists_ resolve to the wrong field
FALSIFY: Length-prefix each component to prevent cross-boundary collisions
LOOK FOR: Hash computations concatenating variable-length fields without separators
cards.antipattern: No length delimiters between components
cards.securePattern: Length-prefix each component to prevent cross-boundary collisions
cards.validWhen: The hash_type_and_key function concatenates parent, k_bytes, and k_tag_bytes without inserting length delimiters.
cards.invalidWhen: Length-prefix each component to prevent cross-boundary collisions
